title: Respiratory Distress in Preterm - RDS
chief_complaint: Preterm male born at 32 weeks via emergency C-section for maternal preeclampsia. Birth weight 1800g. Grunting, nasal flaring, and retractions at 2 hours of life. Room air SpO2 82%.

physical_exam.chest: Bilateral grunting, nasal flaring, severe intercostal and subcostal retractions, decreased air entry bilaterally

hidden_data.cxr: Diffuse reticulogranular pattern with air bronchograms, low lung volumes

patient_responses.pregnancy: Emergency C-section at 32 weeks for severe preeclampsia
patient_responses.antenatal_steroids: She received one dose of betamethasone 4 hours before delivery

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Classic RDS in preterm infant.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is RDS. The prematurity + CXR findings are diagnostic.
feedback.attending_note: RDS management: CPAP 5-8 cmH2O, surfactant replacement therapy if FiO2 >0.4. Maintain SpO2 90-95%. Caffeine for apnea of prematurity. Good catch on the antenatal steroid history.
